title: Early English Poems and Lives of Saints: source lineage for CME00116
summary: CME00116 is a repository copy of U-M's electronic encoding of Furnivall's thirty-six-item edited selection. The XML represents a Transactions 1858 Part II issue state published in Berlin in 1862; a complete cached Internet Archive scan preserves the related standalone 1862 title-page state and full apparatus. Furnivall transcribed items 1–7 and 32–36 from Harley MS 913, item 8 from one Egerton MS 613 copy with the later copy collated, items 9–24 from Harley MS 2277, items 25–30 from Add MS 22283 with Vernon used only for item 25, and item 31 from Cotton MS Vespasian D IX. A complete ORA Vernon facsimile is cached under ORA's one-copy private-study/research terms; complete public BL IIIF objects for Harley 913 and Egerton 613 remain uncached because reusable bulk-download permission was not established.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00116
entities.description: The checked-in XML artifact consumed by the repository workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00116; the file is 413,452 bytes.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00116
entities.description: A public electronic text encoding an issue half-title, the Transactions 1858 title leaf, and Furnivall's thirty-six numbered medieval-text selections on printed pp. 1–161. It omits the standalone 1862 title page, preface, contents, corrigenda, appended notes, index, and list of words.

entities.label: Transactions of the Philological Society 1858, Part II: Early English Poems
entities.description: The immediate source-edition state represented by the XML. Its encoded preliminaries read 'The Early South-English Legendary or Lives of Saints' and 'Transactions of the Philological Society 1858'; DIMEV identifies Furnivall's part as Berlin 1862. The text comprises thirty-six selected items on pp. 1–161. The precise physical issue exemplar used for encoding remains unidentified.
alternate_labels: The Early South-English Legendary or Lives of Saints

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Early English Poems, Transactions of the Philological Society 1858, Part II (Berlin: A. Asher & Co., 1862), pp. 1–180.
bibliographic.title: Early English Poems
bibliographic.publisher: A. Asher & Co. for the Philological Society

notes: The XML selection ends at p. 161; Furnivall's appended comparative text and apparatus continue through p. 180.
The issue's volume year and the part's publication year are recorded separately rather than collapsed.

entities.label: Frederick J. Furnivall, Early English Poems and Lives of Saints (1862 standalone title-page state)
entities.description: The complete standalone presentation preserved by the Internet Archive exemplar: title page, preface signed 17 December 1861, contents, corrigenda, selected texts on pp. 1–161, notes on pp. 162–165, index on pp. 166–171, and list of words on pp. 172–180. Its main text corresponds to the issue-state selection, but its preliminary leaves differ from the XML.
alternate_labels: Early English Poems and Lives of Saints (with Those of the Wicked Birds Pilate and Judas)

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Early English Poems and Lives of Saints (with Those of the Wicked Birds Pilate and Judas) (Berlin: A. Asher & Co. for the Philological Society, 1862).
bibliographic.title: Early English Poems and Lives of Saints (with Those of the Wicked Birds Pilate and Judas)
bibliographic.publisher: A. Asher & Co. for the Philological Society

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Furnivall's 1862 standalone volume
entities.description: A complete 226-image provider object from the Bavarian State Library exemplar, with image-container PDF, plain OCR, page-level DjVu XML, ScanData, and item metadata. The scan contains duplicated capture leaves for printed pp. 54–55; the physical printed sequence itself remains pp. 1–180.
entities.reproduction_format: 226-image scan object with PDF and OCR derivatives

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 913
entities.description: The Kildare Lyrics codex and Furnivall's direct source for items 1–7 and 32–36. The selected pieces occupy discontiguous loci across ff. 3–63; Harley 913 is one physical witness, not evidence that the selected works share one author.

physical_description.format: Codex in Gothic cursive; Middle English, Old French, and Latin
notes: Probably produced in south-east Ireland, perhaps Waterford, in a Franciscan context.

entities.label: British Library complete IIIF facsimile of Harley MS 913
entities.description: The provider's complete 145-canvas IIIF Presentation 3 object. Its canvas IDs return HTTP 400 when dereferenced directly, while the manifest, viewer, and Image API URLs work unauthenticated. No local bulk copy was created because the manifest's rights URL is currently broken and reusable bulk-download terms were not established.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 3 manifest with 145 image canvases

entities.label: British Library, Egerton MS 613
entities.description: A composite thirteenth-century miscellany containing two copies of the Conduct of Life. Furnivall printed the earlier copy at ff. 7r–12v and recorded readings from the later incomplete copy at ff. 64r–70v; both belong to one codex but have distinct scribal and chronological contexts.

date_statements.label: Relevant manuscript sections
date_statements.value: Thirteenth century; later copy in the first half of the thirteenth century

physical_description.format: Composite codex copied by eleven scribes
notes: Probable south-west Midlands origin; the principal copy is ff. 7r–12v and the later incomplete copy is ff. 64r–70v.

entities.label: British Library complete IIIF facsimile of Egerton MS 613
entities.description: The provider's complete 197-canvas IIIF Presentation 3 object. Its canvas IDs return HTTP 400 when dereferenced directly, while the manifest, viewer, and Image API URLs work unauthenticated. No local bulk copy was created because the manifest supplies only a territorial public-domain statement, not a reusable license or clear bulk-download permission.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 3 manifest with 197 image canvases

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2277
entities.description: The c. 1300 South English Legendary witness from which Furnivall selected items 9–24. The manuscript is imperfect at the beginning; current catalog evidence places production in south-west England, and DIMEV/LALME associate its language with Somerset.

physical_description.format: Codex in Gothic cursive; South English Legendary on ff. 1r–232v, imperfect at beginning
notes: Current catalog origin: south-west England.
DIMEV reports Pilate through f. 234, which conflicts with the current cataloged physical extent through f. 232v.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 22283 (Simeon Manuscript)
entities.description: The late-fourteenth-century Simeon Manuscript and Furnivall's base for items 25–30 at ff. 128–130. It is closely related to the Vernon Manuscript and shares its principal scribe, but only item 25 was collated against Vernon.

physical_description.format: Large religious and literary codex in Middle English, French, and Latin
notes: Probably produced in or associated with north Worcestershire; Scribe 2 also copied most of the Vernon Manuscript.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS. Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)
entities.description: The c. 1390–1400 Vernon Manuscript. Its copy of the Song of Mercy at f. 407ra–c supplied Furnivall with four lines and variant readings for item 25 only. It is a collateral comparison witness, not the base of items 25–30.

physical_description.extent: 8 + 342 surviving medieval folios plus modern leaves; original extent 8 + 413 folios
physical_description.format: Very large illustrated codex with Middle English, Anglo-Norman, and Latin texts
notes: Current catalog date c. 1390–1400 and probable West Midlands origin.
The Song of Mercy is f. 407ra–c.

entities.label: ORA complete electronic facsimile edition of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: A publicly downloadable 4.3 GB ZIP containing a full-color high-resolution facsimile, complete live transcription, page descriptions, and essays. One complete local research copy is cached under ORA's private-study/research and noncommercial-use terms and is not licensed for redistribution.
entities.reproduction_format: 4.3 GB downloadable electronic facsimile and transcription ZIP

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Vespasian D IX
entities.description: The mid-fifteenth-century witness of Why I Can't Be a Nun. The poem is imperfect at beginning and end across ff. 177–182, with a damaged continuation misbound as f. 190; the two catalog components belong to the same medieval manuscript text.

physical_description.format: Composite codex; the target occupies ff. 177–182 and misbound f. 190
notes: The target is imperfect; f. 190 is damaged at the upper outer area and dislocated from ff. 177–182.

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 component, witness, and bibliography records for CME00116
entities.description: The versioned DIMEV data release records thirty-seven separately indexed verse works or components corresponding to Furnivall's thirty-six numbered items, with IMEV/NIMEV identifiers, forms, witness loci, and bibliography. Item 6 contains two DIMEV records.

rights.component: Modern XML markup and other repository material covered by the pinned CME root LICENSE
rights.basis: The pinned Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE is the Unlicense and dedicates covered repository software to the public domain. This statement applies only to repository-authored material to the extent the root license covers it; it does not transfer that status to the underlying medieval text, Furnivall's edition or apparatus, U-M material outside the repository artifact, catalog data, or any provider image.
notes: The root license speaks of software; its application to every non-software data layer is therefore not broadened beyond covered repository material.

rights.component: Medieval Middle English and Latin textual content represented in the edition
rights.basis: The selected textual works are directly attested in thirteenth- through fifteenth-century manuscripts, centuries before the modern copyright term. This work-level conclusion is independent of U-M's more conservative No Copyright–United States provider statement and does not govern modern photographs, catalog text, editions, markup, or provider interfaces.

rights.component: Furnivall's 1862 Transactions issue-state edition text and editorial apparatus
rights.basis: This issue-state edition and its apparatus were published in Berlin in 1862, despite the containing Transactions volume year 1858. The publication date independently places the nineteenth-century edition material in the United States public domain; this claim does not govern later encodings or reproductions.

rights.component: Furnivall's 1862 edition text and editorial apparatus
rights.basis: The standalone title-page state and its editorial apparatus were published in Berlin in 1862, independently placing the nineteenth-century edition material in the United States public domain. This claim does not govern later encodings or scan derivatives.

rights.component: U-M electronic text and encoded edition content
rights.basis: The official item displays Public Domain language and links the RightsStatements.org No Copyright–United States statement. The manifest conservatively maps that provider assertion to no known copyright rather than an independent public-domain legal conclusion.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan and OCR derivatives
rights.basis: The exact item metadata applies Creative Commons Public Domain Mark 1.0 via licenseurl. The manifest conservatively records this provider-applied status mark as no known copyright rather than independently adjudicating every scan and OCR derivative as public domain.
notes: A public-domain mark is a status label, not a license or a grant of rights in unrelated manuscript images.

rights.basis: The exact manifest's rights field points to a British Library reuse page that returned HTTP 404 during final audit; no item-level reusable license or bulk-download permission was established.
notes: Public viewing access is not treated as redistribution permission.

rights.basis: The exact manifest says 'Public Domain in most countries, other than the UK' but supplies no license. The territorial statement is preserved without converting it into unrestricted reuse permission.

rights.component: 2019 electronic facsimile, transcription, interface, and essays
rights.basis: The ORA item names Bodleian Library Publishing as copyright holder, dated 2019. ORA's default terms allow one copy for private study or research for noncommercial purposes, do not authorize redistribution, and prohibit profit-making use absent another license.
notes: This statement is separate from the public-domain medieval text depicted.

rights.basis: The release LICENSE and README apply Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Five principal manuscripts containing many more texts
editorial_practices.representation: Thirty-six numbered selections ordered by Furnivall
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall deliberately selected short poems, South English Legendary lives and miracles, songs, one anti-nunnery satire, and Kildare Lyrics; adjacency in the printed anthology is editorial and does not create a shared medieval codex or author.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Ordinary manuscript contractions and scribal flourishes
editorial_practices.representation: Contractions expanded; some final s/es in italic type
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall says ordinary contractions were expanded and explains that some italic final s/es either aid rhythm or represent a scribal flourish.

editorial_practices.representation: Editor-added commas and other printed punctuation
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall explicitly states that he added commas at times; printed punctuation must not be transferred to manuscript hands.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two Conduct of Life copies in Egerton MS 613
editorial_practices.representation: Principal copy in the text with 258 XML NOTE elements recording later-copy readings
editorial_practices.description: Item 8 uses ff. 7r–12v as its direct base and ff. 64r–70v as a same-codex comparison witness.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Add MS 22283 base and Vernon MS f. 407ra–c
editorial_practices.representation: Four Vernon lines supplied and eighty variant notes recorded
editorial_practices.description: The Vernon comparison applies only to item 25. Furnivall explicitly says he did not have time to compare item 26 and the following songs with the Bodleian copy.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Issue/standalone volume with preliminaries, thirty-six-item text, and apparatus through p. 180
editorial_practices.representation: Issue half-title and title leaf plus printed pp. 1–161 encoded
editorial_practices.description: The electronic item encodes the medieval-text selection but omits the standalone title page, preface, contents, corrigenda, appended comparison on pp. 162–164, notes, index, and list of words.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME, pinned repository root LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: The pinned root LICENSE is the Unlicense: covered repository software is dedicated to the public domain and supplied without warranty. No path-specific license or rights notice was found under CME/source, so the statement is kept at the covered repository-material layer and is not propagated to underlying texts, editions, catalog records, or images.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00116.xml.
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD, IDG, FRONT, BODY/DIV1 and all thirty-six DIV2 descendants
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for repository identity, U-M identifier, issue half-title and title leaf, thirty-six-item order, manuscript citations, selected medieval text, printed-page milestones, notes, supplied matter, and 2016 review history.

evidence.citation: Reproducible structural, pagination, note-distribution, numbering, and scan-boundary audit of CME00116.xml.
evidence.locator: Element counts; all DIV2 first/final lines; PB @N/@REF; LG/L/MILESTONE/NOTE distributions; comparison with ScanData and DjVu OCR
evidence.summary: Confirms 413,452 bytes; three DIV1, thirty-six DIV2, 353 HEAD, 348 LG, 5,615 L, 375 NOTE, 62 P, and 163 body PB elements; exact item page spans; note concentrations in items 8 and 25; numbering resets and starred offsets; and no separate PB for item 36. Scan comparison confirms work boundaries and duplicate capture leaves for printed pp. 54–55.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English item CME00116.
evidence.summary: Official current evidence for title, Furnivall contributor heading, Berlin 1862 publication, public full text, and Public Domain / No Copyright–United States statement. Both URLs returned HTTP 403 to unauthenticated command-line requests during final audit, so structure and content claims are independently grounded in the checked-in XML and scanned edition.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Early English Poems and Lives of Saints (Berlin: A. Asher & Co. for the Philological Society, 1862).
evidence.locator: standalone title PDF p. 5; preface PDF pp. 7–10; contents PDF pp. 11–34; corrigenda PDF p. 35; text printed pp. 1–161 / PDF pp. 37–199; apparatus printed pp. 162–180 / PDF pp. 200–218
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for exact standalone title, Furnivall's editor/compiler role, five principal manuscripts, Vernon comparison only for Song of Mercy, transcription and punctuation practices, item order, and complete printed-volume boundaries. The preface is signed 17 December 1861.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item metadata for EarlyEnglishPoemsLivesOfSaints.
evidence.locator: metadata object and exact PDF, OCR, DjVu XML, and ScanData file inventory
evidence.summary: Confirms identifier, Furnivall title and creator, 1862 date, 226-image Bavarian State Library source object, exact derivative filenames and byte sizes, public download, and Creative Commons Public Domain Mark 1.0.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive ScanData and page-level DjVu OCR for EarlyEnglishPoemsLivesOfSaints.
evidence.locator: 226 pageData objects; BookReader leaves n36–n198; duplicate printed pp. 54–55 capture leaves
evidence.summary: Establishes the exact work boundary at BookReader leaves n36–n198 / printed pp. 1–161 and distinguishes reproduction-layer duplicate captures of pp. 54–55 from XML pagination and printed-text sequence.
notes: The separately cached DjVu XML has SHA-256 5fff8e6fec5fd821fcc6ea111643a3d74e329f42f772fbd5ffd2fde11920368b.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, thirty-seven records corresponding to Furnivall's thirty-six numbered items.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml targeted records; data/MSSIndex.xml six witness keys; data/Bibliography.xml Furnivall1858-62
evidence.summary: Versioned evidence for component titles and incipits, IMEV/NIMEV identifiers, verse forms, witness loci, imperfect or excerpt status, and Furnivall bibliography. It confirms Vernon Song of Mercy at f. 407ra–c and Add MS 22283 at f. 128vb–129ra. The release is CC BY 4.0.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 913: Kildare Lyrics.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for exact shelfmark, mid-fourteenth-century 1330–1370 date, probable south-east Irish/Waterford origin, possible Franciscan context, parchment extent, Gothic cursive hand, languages, complete digitized-content link, and physical request route.

evidence.citation: British Library complete IIIF Presentation 3 manifest for Harley MS 913.
evidence.locator: 145 canvases; selected range begins f. 3r and ends f. 63v; all relevant discontiguous loci checked against labels
evidence.summary: Confirms a complete public codex viewer and exact working Image API boundaries. Manifest canvas IDs returned HTTP 400, while manifest, viewer, and Image API links returned HTTP 200. The manifest rights URL currently returns 404, so the object was not bulk-cached.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Egerton MS 613.
evidence.summary: Current official parent record for the composite thirteenth-century parchment miscellany, its 74-folio extent, probable south-west Midlands origin, eleven scribes, section dates, complete digitized-content link, and physical request route.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Egerton MS 613, ff. 7r–12v: Conduct of Life.
evidence.summary: Official component evidence for the principal Conduct of Life copy, exact locus ff. 7r–12v, Middle English incipit, thirteenth-century date, six parchment leaves, and DIMEV/IMEV identification.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Egerton MS 613, ff. 64r–70v: Conduct of Life.
evidence.summary: Official component evidence for the later incomplete copy used in Furnivall's variant apparatus, exact locus ff. 64r–70v, first-half-thirteenth-century date, and Middle English incipit.

evidence.citation: British Library complete IIIF Presentation 3 manifest for Egerton MS 613.
evidence.locator: 197 canvases; principal copy ff. 7r–12v and collation copy ff. 64r–70v
evidence.summary: Confirms a complete public codex viewer and exact working Image API boundaries for both Conduct of Life copies. Canvas IDs returned HTTP 400; manifest, viewer, and Image API links returned HTTP 200. The manifest states 'Public Domain in most countries, other than the UK' without a license, so the object was not bulk-cached.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2277: South English Legendary.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for c. 1300 production in south-west England, parchment extent of 232 folios, Gothic cursive script, imperfect South English Legendary contents, physical request route, and an unavailable legacy digitized viewer.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 22283: Simeon Manuscript.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for exact shelfmark, late-fourteenth-century date, probable north Worcestershire association, 172-folio parchment codex, relationship to the Vernon Manuscript through a shared scribe, physical request route, and unavailable legacy images.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Vespasian D IX, ff. 177–182 and f. 190: Why I Can't Be a Nun.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the two physical components of the same imperfect Middle English poem, mid-fifteenth-century 1430–1470 date, English origin, separated f. 190 relation, absence of current digitized content, and physical request route.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services.
evidence.summary: The public online service accepts paid reference- or reproduction-image orders from individuals and researchers. It requires a shelfmark, title, exact folios and sides, desired format, and payment; precious or rare manuscripts may require expert photography, and supply remains subject to condition and feasibility.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: The January 2026 terms make image supply conditional on availability, preservation review, payment, and service timing; users must clear relevant intellectual-property rights, and copying or reproduction of supplied images may require written permission and additional fees.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MARCO and Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Eng. poet. a. 1: Vernon Manuscript.
evidence.locator: whole codex; Song of Mercy at f. 407ra–c from DIMEV crosswalk
evidence.summary: Official evidence for exact shelfmark, c. 1390–1400 date, probable West Midlands origin, parchment extent and losses, languages, hands, decoration, and Scribe 2's identity with the principal Simeon Manuscript scribe.

evidence.citation: Wendy Scase and Nick Kennedy, A Facsimile Edition of the Vernon Manuscript, ORA dataset (2019).
evidence.locator: Complete 4.3 GB ZIP; target Song of Mercy at manuscript f. 407ra–c
evidence.summary: Official record states that the package contains full-color high-resolution images of the entire manuscript, a complete live transcription, page descriptions, and essays. The exact ZIP returned HTTP 200 unauthenticated; the cached 4,616,920,131-byte archive passed a complete ZIP integrity test. Bodleian Library Publishing is named copyright holder, dated 2019.

evidence.citation: Oxford University Research Archive, Terms and Conditions of Use.
evidence.summary: Unless an item has a different permission notice, ORA permits downloading or printing one copy for private study or research for noncommercial purposes; redistribution and profit-making use are not permitted absent another license. Item links should use the record page.

open_questions.question: Which physical Transactions issue exemplar supplied the XML's half-title and 1858 title leaf, and what is its exact relationship to the standalone 1862 volume?
next_steps: Ask U-M for source-copy catalog and imaging provenance.
Locate a complete Transactions 1858 Part II issue and compare preliminaries, signatures, pagination, and appended matter against the XML and cached scan.
Keep the volume year 1858 separate from the Berlin publication year 1862 until the issue history is resolved.

open_questions.question: Can complete stable page-image runs with explicit research and reuse terms be obtained for Harley MS 2277, Add MS 22283, and Cotton MS Vespasian D IX?
next_steps: Ask British Library Reference Services whether legacy digitization survives and request working manifests or complete target runs.
If commissioned, include adjacent leaves, blanks, dislocated f. 190, bindings, and foliation evidence; obtain order-specific reuse terms.
Do not infer nondigitization from currently unavailable legacy viewers.

open_questions.question: How should DIMEV's Harley MS 2277 locus through f. 234 be reconciled with the current BL codex extent through f. 232v for Pilate?
next_steps: Inspect complete final-leaf images or request a current-versus-legacy foliation concordance from the British Library.
Compare Furnivall's printed text and any old foliation against DIMEV's source record before changing the locus.

open_questions.question: What exact reusable license or permission applies to bulk local research copies of the complete Harley MS 913 and Egerton MS 613 IIIF objects?
next_steps: Ask the British Library to repair or replace Harley 913's manifest rights URL and clarify bulk-download permission.
Obtain a jurisdiction-specific reuse statement or license for Egerton 613 beyond the territorial public-domain note.
Continue using public viewer and tested Image API boundaries without bulk-caching until terms are clear.

review_notes: The lineage primary_subject is the repository artifact entity artifact:general-corpus:CME00116, which carries repository_file.path; no abstract work or source edition is used as the primary subject.

The five principal physical witnesses and the Vernon comparison witness remain separate. Item 8's two Egerton copies are separately scoped within one codex; item 31's dislocated f. 190 remains part of the Cotton witness.

Complete public access and local caching are separate assertions: Harley 913 and Egerton 613 are online and complete but remain uncached because reusable bulk-download permission was not established; Vernon is cached as the one private-study/research copy allowed by ORA terms and remains non-redistributable.
Every cached complete source object records the corpus work portion and tested boundaries where a human-facing deep link exists.
